metadata.title: My Flow Check | 3-Day Bladder Diary Tracker
metadata.description: Track when you drink, when you pee, and the patterns behind it in 3 days. A simple bladder diary tool to help you and your health professional.
metadata.ogImageAlt: My Flow Check, a 3-day bladder diary tracker

landing.sessionInProgress: You have a tracking session in progress.
landing.resetWarning: This will delete all your current entries. Make sure you have exported your data first.
landing.heroTitle: Track your drinks and bathroom habits in 3 days
landing.heroSubtitle: Track when you drink, when you pee, and see useful patterns. All in 3 days.
landing.pwaPromptAndroid: Want quick access? You can add My Flow Check to your home screen — no app store needed. Or just keep using it in your browser.
landing.pwaPromptIos: Want quick access? Tap the share button at the bottom of your screen, then "Add to Home Screen". Or just keep using it right here in your browser.
landing.privacyBody: Your data never leaves your phone. No account needed, no sign-up, nothing stored on our servers. When you're done, you choose what to share with your health professional.
landing.disclaimer: This app is intended for personal tracking and educational purposes only. It does not provide medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Always consult a qualified health professional regarding any medical condition or health concerns.

onboarding.ageSubtitle: This is useful information for your health professional.
onboarding.unitSubtitle: Choose the unit you're most comfortable with. This will be used throughout the app.
onboarding.dateTitle: When do you want to start?
onboarding.dateSubtitle: You need to track for 3 consecutive days for it to work. Start on a morning when you can record your first pee of the day.

timeline.overnightQuestion: Did you pee or drink anything overnight?
timeline.useInsertButton: Use the + button to log overnight events, or continue to the next day
timeline.addOvernightFirst: Add all overnight pees and drinks before marking wake-up
timeline.addBeforeBedtime: Add all pees and drinks before marking bedtime
timeline.clearAllWarning: This will clear all data from Day 1, Day 2, and Day 3. This can't be undone.
timeline.addFirstEvent: Add your first event of the day

logVoid.doubleVoidDescription: Peed again right after to fully empty
logVoid.secondPeeAmount: How much was the second pee?
logVoid.optionalTapHint: Optional — tap to select, tap again to clear

logLeak.optionalTapHint: optional — tap to select, tap again to clear

bedtime.whenGoToBed: When did you go to bed?
bedtime.afterWakeUp: Bedtime must be after your wake-up at {time}
bedtime.afterLastEvent: Bedtime must be after your last event at {time}. Overnight events go on the next day.

wakeTime.afterBedtime: Wake-up must be after last night's bedtime at {time}
wakeTime.beforeBedtime: Wake-up must be before your bedtime at {time}

validation.beforePrevBedtime: This time is before Day {dayNumber}'s bedtime ({time}). Pick a later time.
validation.beforeWakeUp: This time is before wake-up ({time}). Daytime events must be after you woke up.
validation.afterWakeUp: This time is after wake-up ({time}). Overnight events must be before you woke up.
validation.afterBedtime: This time is after bedtime ({time}). Go to the night view for overnight events.

toilet_way.description: I couldn't quite make it to the toilet

summary.diaryLockedDescription: Complete your 3-day tracking period to unlock your diary and export your data.
summary.reviewDataSuffix: . You can still generate your report, but for the most accurate results consider going back to add any missing entries.
summary.noDrinkEntries: Day {day} has no drink entries - your report will still work but fluid intake data will be missing
summary.ipcDescription: Better data leads to better care. Share these results with your health professional at your next appointment.

export.noDataYet: Start adding entries to enable export

milestones.firstEventSubtitle: Your data is saved on this device — come back anytime
milestones.day1CompleteSubtitle: Great job — 2 more days to go
milestones.day2CompleteSubtitle: You're over halfway — keep it up!
milestones.day3CompleteSubtitle: Tap View Results to see your diary

notifications.morningBody: Don't forget to add your wake-up time and first pee of the day.
notifications.afternoonBody: Have you been adding your drinks and pees? It only takes a second.
notifications.eveningBody: When you're ready to sleep, tap "Go to bed" to wrap up your day.
notifications.completeBody: Your 3-day flow check is complete. Open the app to see your summary.

ipcModal.title: Integrated Pelvic Care
ipcModal.description: At IPC, we believe better data leads to better care. We specialise in male pelvic health and are committed to raising the standard of care in this space. Our tools are designed for everyone, because good health tracking should be accessible to all.
ipcModal.coursesNote: Courses and resources for health professionals

help.title: Help & FAQ
help.description: Frequently asked questions about My Flow Check. Learn how to track drinks, bathroom visits, and understand your 3-day bladder diary.
help.a1: It's a simple 3-day tool to track when you drink, when you pee, and when you go to bed. This helps your health professional spot patterns and recommend the best next steps for you.
help.q2: How do I add a pee?
help.a2: Tap the + button at the bottom of the screen, then tap "Pee". Use the slider to enter how much, rate how strong the urge was, and tap Save.
help.q3: How do I measure how much?
help.a3: Your health professional may provide a measuring jug. Pee into the jug, read the volume in millilitres, then enter it in the app. For reference: a small glass is about 150 mL, a standard glass is 250 mL.
help.q4: What do the urge levels mean?
help.a4: Not at all = you went just in case. A little = slight feeling. Normal = comfortable need. Quite a bit = hard to hold. Couldn't wait = urgent, may have leaked.
help.q5: Why should I add my bedtime?
help.a5: Adding your bedtime helps your health professional tell the difference between daytime and nighttime pees. Waking up at night to pee may need a different approach.
help.a6: Go to the Summary tab to review your data. You can download a PDF or CSV file to share with your health professional at your next appointment.
help.a7: Yes. All your data is stored only on your phone and is never sent to any server. When you export your data, only you decide who to share it with.
help.q8: Can I edit or delete an entry?
help.a8: Yes. On the day timeline, tap the trash icon next to any entry to delete it. You can then re-add it with the correct information.
help.q9: What if I forget to add something?
help.a9: No worries! You can add entries at any time and adjust the time to when it actually happened. Just tap the + button and change the time before saving.
help.stillHaveQuestions: Still have questions? Ask your health professional.
help.suggestionBody: We'd love to hear your ideas for improving the app. Send us an email at
